questions.prompt: Which site regions should become living slots? The home page hero is the obvious first candidate. What else should evolve?
questions.description: Slots are named regions on kyanitelabs.tech pages where Liminal injects creative output. Each slot runs its own A/B evolution cycle. Start small — we can always add more slots later.
questions.recommendedAnswer: home-hero (the main visual on the landing page) as the first slot. Expand to about-background and blog-header once the system proves stable.

questions.prompt: How often should the evolution daemon run a generation cycle?
questions.description: Each cycle: generate variants → render & score → deploy challenger → check PostHog results → promote winner. More frequent = faster evolution but needs more traffic for statistical significance.
questions.recommendedAnswer: Every 6 hours. Fast enough to feel alive, slow enough to collect meaningful data per variant.

questions.prompt: How aggressively should Liminal explore wild/novel variants vs. refining what works?
questions.description: High novelty = the site changes dramatically and sometimes surprisingly. Low novelty = the site converges on what performs best and evolves subtly. This is the exploration/exploitation tradeoff.
questions.recommendedAnswer: Balanced with periodic wildcards — mostly refine winners, but every few cycles generate something completely unexpected.

questions.prompt: Which Liminal generators should be available for slot filling?
questions.description: Each slot can accept output from any of these domains. More domains = more variety but more complex rendering.
questions.recommendedAnswer: All of them — the whole point is Liminal decides what works best across all its creative tools.

questions.prompt: Where on the VPS should Liminal-generated variant files be served from?
questions.description: Needs to be a directory nginx can serve as static files. The daemon writes HTML here, nginx serves it.
questions.recommendedAnswer: /var/www/kyanitelabs/liminal-asset/ — alongside the existing site root, served under the same domain.

questions.prompt: What engagement signals should determine a 'winning' variant?
questions.description: These are the metrics PostHog tracks per variant. They become the engagement fitness axis that feeds back into Liminal's evolution.
questions.recommendedAnswer: Dwell time (>10s), scroll depth, and any click/interaction. Bounce rate as a negative signal.

options.label: Return visits (visitor comes back within 7 days)

questions.prompt: Minimum visitors per variant before declaring an A/B winner?
questions.description: Too few = unreliable results. Too many = slow evolution. PostHog recommends ~200 minimum for low-effect experiments.
questions.recommendedAnswer: 200 visitors per variant minimum. Good statistical power without making the cycle too slow.

questions.prompt: For the first working demo, should we wire one slot end-to-end first or build all infrastructure at once?
questions.description: End-to-end one slot = you see it working faster. All infrastructure = complete system but longer before anything is visible.
questions.recommendedAnswer: One slot end-to-end first (home-hero). Prove the loop works, then scale.
